H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: Pebble (n=95, mean=7.15, std=1.78, strong confidence) has library's largest n with mode at 8 (26/95) and 66% positive. Qualitative data (n=29) shows noticeable improvement (12/29) with 10-minute settle. Driving/commuting context dominates. YouTube highest reach (30 views) suggests emerging efficacy signal.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: Pebble is very effective for overstimulation relief with broad listener appeal, showing strong real-world use in commute/relaxation contexts.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: Rainfall (n=51, mean=6.92, std=1.73, strong confidence) shows tight distribution with mode at 7 (14/51). 65% positive but mixed music opinion (6 neutral). Settle time splits (10 minutes for 10/21). 5 YouTube views.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Rainfall helps manage overstimulation reliably across listeners, with effects developing within ten minutes for most.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: Glacier (n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68, meaningful confidence) consistent with L1/L2 (mode at 7). Notable for sleep context (8/23 trying to sleep) suggesting cross-zone use. Rhythm opinion evenly split (10 balanced vs 10 very settling).
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Glacier is an effective overstimulation tool that unexpectedly supports sleep induction for some listeners.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: Meadow (n=67, mean=7.22, std=1.93, strong confidence) shows rightward distribution with modes at 6 and 9-10 (63% positive). Qualitative data indicates moderate-to-significant improvement (10/23) with 10-minute settle time, used heavily in care-giving contexts. AutoGluon predicts 7.838 for anxiety zone (vs observed 7.22). YouTube reach minimal (7 views).
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: Meadow helps most listeners reduce anxiety effectively, especially caregivers supporting others. Effects are reliable with balanced rhythm perception.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: Cottage (n=48, mean=6.94, std=1.62, meaningful confidence) shows tighter distribution than L1 with peak at 7 (15/48). 67% positive outcomes but notable neutral music opinion (5/20). Settle times vary (5-20 minutes). Drift score 0.652 suggests analytical narrative shifts. YouTube data at 6 views.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: Cottage provides solid anxiety relief for most, though some find the music style neutral and effects take longer to settle.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: Valley (n=36, mean=7.08, std=1.89, meaningful confidence) has flatter distribution than L2 with tail to 10 (5/36). Qualitative data (n=20) shows noticeable improvement (8/20) and 10-minute settle time. Listener mix includes 6 carers/supporters suggesting proxy scoring. No YouTube data.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: Valley supports anxiety reduction well, particularly for those who listen fully. Effects may develop gradually rather than immediately.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: Anchor (n=43, mean=6.35, std=1.70, meaningful confidence) shows structurally lower scores expected for crisis states with central cluster at 8 but heavy 4-7 mass. Qualitative data (n=24) reveals significant improvement (8/24) despite modest mean, settling in 5 minutes. AutoGluon cross-bucket routes to X-ME-L1/L2.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: Anchor effectively supports acute distress with rapid calming effects, though outcomes vary given the high-stress arrival states.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: Harbor (n=33, mean=6.52, std=1.70, meaningful confidence) has similar distribution to L1 but right-shifted (mode at 7). Split music opinion (6 neutral, 5 loved) may reflect crisis-state sensitivity. Used in working/sleep contexts. AutoGluon predicts 7.955 (vs observed 6.52).
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: Harbor offers reliable crisis relief with calming rhythm, though opinions on the music style vary significantly.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: Horizon (n=27, mean=6.52, std=1.91, meaningful confidence) shows higher spread with scores to 10 (3/27). 7/19 settle immediately, suggesting rapid-responder subgroup, but 4/19 disliked music. YouTube data at 5 views.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: Horizon provides breakthrough support in distress moments with some listeners settling immediately, but small sample size limits certainty.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: Lantern (n=54, mean=7.19, std=1.86, strong confidence) shows strong rightward distribution with 11 scores at 9 and 5 at 10. Qualitative data (n=15) shows 5-minute settle times and noticeable improvement (6/15). YouTube reach negligible (4 views).
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: Lantern effectively supports sleep preparation with high efficacy and rapid settling for most listeners.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: Hammock (n=45, mean=7.09, std=1.55, meaningful confidence) has tight distribution with modes at 7 and 8. Qualitative data unusual: 11/15 are clinicians/carers (not direct listeners). 10-minute settle time dominant but 3/15 report 'Too busy' rhythm.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Hammock provides good sleep support with less pronounced musical features that suit clinical and dependent support contexts.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: Midnight (n=28, mean=7.07, std=1.68, meaningful confidence) shows central cluster at 7-8 lacking right tail. Mixed listener base (7 carers, 3 clinicians, 12 self) with 7/22 reporting 'Slightly busy' rhythm. Settle splits (8 at 5 min, 5 at 10 min).
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: Midnight offers reliable sleep support with interesting variance between direct listeners and specialist observers.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: Compass (n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82, meaningful confidence) shows distribution from 3-10 with peak at 7. 9/23 report 'Slightly busy' rhythm which may distract during focus tasks. Studying/relaxing contexts. Music strongly positive (15/23 liked).
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: Compass provides reliable focus support but its rhythm is perceived as busy by some listeners.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: Waypoint (n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.60, meaningful confidence) stronger than Compass (mode at 8). Qualitative shows care-giving dominance (5/17), potentially inflating proxy scores. 10-minute settle time (9/17). YouTube sparse (4 views).
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: Waypoint offers strong focus support with musical texture that differs from L1 and appeals to diverse listeners.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: Summit (n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97, meaningful confidence) is library's highest mean but bimodal (10 scores at 10 vs central 5-8 cluster). Qualitative shows 10-minute settle (15/29) and significant improvement (11/29). AutoGluon predicts 7.728. YouTube leads THRIVING (15 views).
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: Summit is one of the strongest focus protocols available with high efficacy across concentration scenarios.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: Ember (n=34, mean=6.74, std=1.73, meaningful confidence) has modest pct_pos (53%) with distribution peaked at 7. Qualitative shows slight improvement (8/21) and immediate settle (6/21). Relaxing/therapy contexts dominate. May benefit from longer hold phase.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: Ember supports creative flow moderately well, though effects are subtle and may require multiple exposures.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: Current (n=24, mean=6.79, std=1.41, meaningful confidence) shows low variance distribution with mode at 7 (8/24). Qualitative stronger (significant improvement 5/15, loved music 6/15). 10-minute settle time (5/15). YouTube at 2 views.
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: Current reliably enhances creative flow with positive listener reception despite modest sample size.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: Aurora (n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32, developing confidence) achieves THRIVING's highest Creative Flow mean with tight spread. Qualitative disproportionate (n=25) with sleep context (5/25). Drift score 0.663 reflects analytical instability. YouTube at 7 views.
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: Aurora is emerging as a strong creative flow tool with consistent response at moderate sample size.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: Stone (n=25, mean=7.04, std=1.34, meaningful confidence) has THRIVING's tightest distribution (no scores below 5). 9/23 require 20-minute settle — longer than most protocols. Therapy context dominant. Music split (6 neutral vs 6 liked).
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: Stone maintains reliable balance with the longest typical settle time in the maintenance protocols.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: Axis (n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03, meaningful confidence) shows THRIVING's highest variance (span 2-10). Listener mix split (7 clinicians vs 7 direct) and rhythm split (7 balanced vs 7 slightly busy) suggest population mismatch.
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: Axis provides consistent maintenance support with noticeable variance between clinicians and direct users.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: Orbit (n=14, mean=6.93, std=1.73, developing confidence) shows gap pattern (score 7 peak, zero at 8-9). 4/19 report non-settle — only explicit rejection signal. Studying/care contexts. 7/19 rhythm balanced, 5 slightly busy.
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: Orbit shows stronger effects for certain groups but needs larger samples to confirm its pattern.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: Breeze (n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94, meaningful confidence) appears in AutoGluon cross-bucket for 9 zones (predicts 8.230 for low zone). Qualitative shows 20-minute settle (6/22) and moderate improvement (7/22). Care-giving context significant (5/22).
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: Breeze provides good mood and energy support with cross-utility beyond its design intent.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: Sunrise (n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71, meaningful confidence) shows strong qualitative (7/15 significant improvement). 4/15 settle immediately, 4/15 at 20 minutes. AutoGluon cross-bucket predicts universal appeal. YouTube at 8 views.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: Sunrise offers similar mood support to Breeze with strong listener reception and 20-minute settle patterns.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: Updraft (n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, developing confidence) shows 5/25 no effect despite 5-minute settle. Qualitative indicates bimodal response. ASCENT may not sustain engagement for all. YouTube minimal (4 views).
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: Updraft is emerging as an interesting mood tool with mixed efficacy at small sample size.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: Footpath (n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01, meaningful confidence) has lowest Motivation & Drive pct_pos (45%). 4/17 score 0-3 suggesting ASCENT arc distresses some listeners. Settle fast (9/17 at 5 min). 6/17 report 'too busy' rhythm.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: Footpath supports motivation moderately well but fails for some listeners who experience distress.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: Trail (n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63, developing confidence) shows low mean but loved music (9/21). 5/21 require 20-minute settle (longest in series). Qualitative shows disconnect between enjoyment and efficacy. YouTube at 7 views.
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: Trail offers moderate motivation support with effects that develop over longer sessions for some.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: Switchback (n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38, developing confidence) inverts series trend with high mean but polarized (3/13 score 9-10, 3/13 score 3-4). Drift extreme (cosine 0.507). Rhythm split (8 balanced, 4 slightly busy).
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: Switchback shows promise with high motivation support for some listeners but polarized effects.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: Cavern (n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62, meaningful confidence) shows moderate distribution with 2 'made things worse' reports (only negative sentiment). Loved music (9/25) but settle time split (8 at 10 min, 8 at 5 min). AutoGluon cross-bucket routes to X-ME-L1/L2.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Cavern supports mind clarity effectively but has the highest number of negative experiences reported.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: Starlight (n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64, developing confidence) shows right-skew with peak at 7. Qualitative strong (significant improvement 7/20, very settling rhythm 12/20). YouTube highest in TRANSFORMING (12 views). AutoGluon predicts 7.538.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: Starlight provides solid clarity support with strong rhythm perception and the highest TRANSFORMING YouTube reach.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: Cosmos (n=10, mean=5.7, std=2.45, developing confidence) is library's lowest mean with 0-2-3-0-2-0 distribution suggesting mismatch. 2/17 report no effect, 1 reports worsening. DESCENT arc may not serve this population. YouTube at 8 views.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: Cosmos currently shows divergent listener experiences with the lowest mean in clarity support.

sphere_commentary.overview:
The data suggests that HEALING protocols achieve the highest confidence in efficacy (mean 6.98, std 1.77), THRIVING shows strong performance in Focus & Clarity (peak 7.63), and TRANSFORMING aligns with design intent through wider variance (mean 6.59, std 1.87). YouTube reach remains undiscovered (max 30 views).
• HEALING dominates sample size (574 responses) with crisis protocols structurally underperforming (6.35-6.52) as predicted
• THRIVING Focus & Clarity L3 (Summit) achieves highest single-protocol mean (7.63) and 71% positive outcomes
• TRANSFORMING ASCENT protocols show bifurcated responses: Mood & Energy clusters (6.28-6.76) while Motivation & Drive flips from low (6.21) to high (7.15)
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
Series efficacy profiles align with design intent: HEALING's descent protocols provide stable regulation, THRIVING's hold protocols excel in optimization, and TRANSFORMING's ascent protocols target low-affect states with expected variability.
• HEALING DESCENT protocols average 6.92 with strong crisis-session support (Anxiety 7.22, Over-stimulation 7.15)
• THRIVING HOLD protocols peak in Focus & Clarity (T-FC-L3 mean 7.63) and Creative Flow (T-CF-L3 mean 7.28)
• TRANSFORMING ASCENT protocols show bimodal responses with Motivation & Drive (6.21-7.15) and Mood & Energy (6.28-6.76) carrying cross-bucket appeal
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
Several protocols show response distributions deviating from series norms, suggesting population subgroups or data patterns warranting investigation.
• X-MC-L3 (Cosmos, n=10, mean=5.7, std=2.45): Lowest-efficacy protocol with extreme spread — potential population mismatch
• T-FC-L3 (Summit, n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97): Bimodal distribution with score-10 cluster and central 5-8 spread — possible subgroup split
• X-MD-L3 (Switchback, n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38): Inverts Motivation & Drive trend with high mean but unstable drift (cosine 0.507)
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Convergent protocol development signals across analyzers.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] — X-ME-L1 (Breeze): Appears in AutoGluon cross-bucket routing across 9 zones; investigate universal appeal beyond Mood & Energy design
• T-FC-L3 (Summit): Bimodal distribution suggests two responder populations; consider L4 variant with adjusted hold-phase intensity
• X-MC-L3 (Cosmos): Mean 5.7 with 0-2-3-0-2-0 distribution indicates possible mismatch; pause expansion pending population investigation
• H-OS-L1 (Pebble): Highest HEALING sample (n=95) with strong efficacy; investigate music style transfer to crisis protocols
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Analytical drift pervades protocol commentary, particularly in TRANSFORMING series, while semantic clustering lacks data volume.
• CROSS-RUN DRIFT: All 30 protocols show cosine similarity <0.85 between runs, with TRANSFORMING protocols most unstable (X-MD-L3 0.507, X-ME-L2 0.532)
• CROSS-PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: Insufficient embeddings for cluster analysis
• The drift pattern is systematic (not protocol-specific) and correlates with developing n and high variance in outcome scores
